text:
Once upon a time in the lush valley of Elderia, there lived a curious fox named Fergus. Fergus loved the secrets the forest held. Every day he would explore hidden glades, climb quiet valleys, and watch the moonlit butterflies dance.

One fateful evening, as Fergus prowled along a silver-lit stream-bed, he heard giggling. It wasn't the kind of giggling a rabbit would cause. Intrigued, he tiptoed closer. There sat a tiny fairy, fluttering her wings.

"What's so laughable?" asked Fergus.

"The starlings. They believe the moon influences love."

"A starling can't love?" Fergus was astonished.

"Ah, well, mostly just their mates seem to believe it. Every month."

"Every month?" Fergus repeated.

"Yes. They think if they stay close on a full moon they'll find true love."

"Really?" Fergus pondered this outlandish idea.
